---
title: "Glossary: Swift, Apple APIs, Models, and Protocols"
---

This glossary is a reference index. The book also defines each term at its
first meaningful use, where its local design consequences are easier to see.

## Swift language

`actor`
: A reference type whose mutable state is isolated by Swift concurrency.

ARC
: Automatic Reference Counting, Swift and Objective-C's compiler-assisted
  strong-reference lifetime system.

`async`
: Marks a function that may suspend. It does not guarantee a background thread
  or make shared state thread-safe.

`AsyncStream<Element>`
: A generic asynchronous sequence whose producer yields values and whose
  consumer iterates them with `for await`.

attribute
: Metadata beginning with `@`, such as `@discardableResult` or `@MainActor`,
  that changes compiler checking or generated behavior.

closure
: An anonymous function value that can capture variables from its surrounding
  scope. `{ $0.id == target }` uses `$0` for its first inferred parameter.

`Codable`
: The composition of Swift's `Encodable` and `Decodable` protocols.

computed property
: A property implemented by accessor code rather than a stored field.

copy-on-write
: Value semantics implemented by sharing storage until one copy mutates.

`enum`
: A compiler-checked sum type. Cases may carry typed payloads, making it
  comparable to a tagged C union.

`Equatable`
: Protocol requiring equality comparison with `==`.

`extension`
: Adds methods or conformances to an existing type without subclassing it.

generic type
: A type parameterized by another type, such as `AsyncStream<Event>`.

`guard`
: An early-exit construct. `guard condition else { return }` guarantees that
  the condition is true for the remainder of the scope.

`init`
: A Swift initializer. Every stored property must be initialized before the
  value becomes usable.

`let`
: Declares an immutable binding or stored property.

`LocalizedError`
: Foundation protocol exposing a human-readable error description.

`mutating`
: Allows a struct or enum method to modify `self`.

`nil`
: The absence case of an `Optional`; it is not an unchecked null pointer.

nil coalescing (`??`)
: Returns an optional's wrapped value or a fallback when it is `nil`.

optional chaining (`?.`)
: Calls a member only when the preceding optional contains a value.

`Optional<T>` / `T?`
: A sum type containing either `.some(T)` or `.none`.

`private(set)`
: Exposes a property getter more widely than its setter.

`protocol`
: A compiler-checked interface contract.

`Sendable`
: Concurrency protocol indicating that a value may cross isolation boundaries.
  It does not serialize mutable implementation state.

`struct`
: A Swift value type. Assignment conceptually copies the value.

`throws`, `throw`, and `try`
: Swift's structured error-propagation mechanism.

trailing closure
: Syntax placing a function's final closure argument after the closing
  parenthesis.

`var`
: Declares a mutable binding or stored property.

`@MainActor`
: Global-actor annotation isolating a declaration to Swift's main executor.

`@Observable`
: Observation-framework macro that synthesizes change tracking used by SwiftUI.

`@State`
: SwiftUI property wrapper providing persistent framework-managed storage to a
  transient view value.

`@unchecked Sendable`
: Manual promise that a type satisfies cross-isolation safety even though the
  compiler cannot prove it.

`@ViewBuilder`
: Result builder used to compose declarative SwiftUI child expressions.

`Binding<T>`
: A typed getter/setter pair exposed as mutable state to SwiftUI controls.

`deinit`
: Finalizer run when the last strong reference to a class instance is released.

`some Protocol`
: Opaque return type hiding one concrete type while preserving static typing.

`Task`
: Unit of Swift asynchronous work with cancellation and task-local context.

`Unmanaged<T>`
: Explicit retain/release interface for passing Swift object references through
  C APIs that do not participate in ARC.

## Application and models

ASR
: Automatic Speech Recognition.

attention KV cache
: Key-value cache retaining transformer attention projections from previous
  positions during autoregressive decoding.

AUHAL
: Audio Unit Hardware Abstraction Layer.

CLI
: Command-Line Interface.

dBFS
: Decibels relative to Full Scale.

DTO
: Data Transfer Object.

FIFO
: First In, First Out.

GPU
: Graphics Processing Unit.

MLX
: Apple's array and machine-learning framework for Apple silicon.

MLX-LM
: Language-model tooling built on MLX.

PCM
: Pulse-Code Modulation.

RMS
: Root Mean Square.

UI
: User Interface.

UUID
: Universally Unique Identifier.

WAV
: Waveform Audio File Format.

FFT
: Fast Fourier Transform.

FP16
: IEEE-754 binary16 half-precision floating-point format.

GELU
: Gaussian Error Linear Unit activation.

MSL
: Metal Shading Language.

MPS
: Metal Performance Shaders.

RMSNorm
: Root Mean Square Layer Normalization.

RoPE
: Rotary Position Embedding.

SiLU
: Sigmoid Linear Unit activation.

SPM
: Swift Package Manager.

STFT
: Short-Time Fourier Transform.

WER
: Word Error Rate.

## Networking and formats

ABI
: Application Binary Interface.

API
: Application Programming Interface.

ASCII
: American Standard Code for Information Interchange.

BF16
: Brain Floating Point, a 16-bit floating-point format.

BLAS
: Basic Linear Algebra Subprograms.

HTTP
: Hypertext Transfer Protocol.

I/O
: Input/Output.

IPv4
: Internet Protocol version 4.

JSON
: JavaScript Object Notation.

little-endian
: Integer byte order storing the least significant byte first.

PC
: Personal Computer.

POST
: HTTP request method conventionally used to submit data.

QR
: Quick Response code.

RIFF
: Resource Interchange File Format, the chunk container used by WAV.

SSE
: Server-Sent Events.

TCP
: Transmission Control Protocol.

URL
: Uniform Resource Locator.

UTF-8
: Unicode Transformation Format, 8-bit form.

Wi-Fi
: Wi-Fi Alliance brand name for IEEE 802.11 wireless networking. It is not an
  acronym for “Wireless Fidelity.”

Base64
: Binary-to-text encoding representing each 24 input bits as four printable
  characters, normally expanding data by roughly one third.

`mmap`
: POSIX operation mapping file-backed bytes into a process's virtual address
  space.

`MTLBuffer`
: Metal byte allocation visible to GPU commands and, depending on storage mode,
  the CPU.

`MTLCommandBuffer`
: Ordered collection of Metal commands submitted to a command queue.

WebSocket
: HTTP-upgraded bidirectional framed transport over one connection.
